prerequisites: A Pexels account (free to create at pexels.com).
A Pexels API key, which an account holder can request from the API section and receives instantly.
A search query string for the `query` config field, since photo/video results are returned per search term (for example "Ocean" or "Tigers").

authMethods.name: API key (Authorization header)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single secret field `api_key`. Pexels expects this key to be sent in the `Authorization` HTTP header on every request. The same key is used for both the photo and video endpoints.

setupSteps.title: Create a Pexels account
setupSteps.body: Sign in to or register a free account at pexels.com. An account is required before an API key can be requested.

setupSteps.title: Request your API key
setupSteps.body: From the Pexels API section, request an API key. According to the official documentation the key is issued instantly to account holders. This value goes into the connector's required `api_key` secret field.

setupSteps.title: Set the search query
setupSteps.body: Provide a value for the required `query` config field. Results are returned for this search term (for example "Ocean" or "Tigers").

setupSteps.title: Optionally narrow results
setupSteps.body: Set any of the optional config fields: `orientation` (landscape, portrait, or square), `size` (large for 24MP, medium for 12MP, or small for 4MP), `color` (one of red, orange, yellow, green, turquoise, blue, violet, pink, brown, black, gray, white, or a hexadecimal code such as #ffffff), and `locale` (a supported locale such as en-US, pt-BR, es-ES, de-DE, fr-FR, ja-JP, zh-CN, among others listed in the docs).

setupSteps.title: Mind the rate limits
setupSteps.body: The default Pexels API limit is 200 requests per hour and 20,000 requests per month. Higher limits are available on request from Pexels with proper attribution.
